score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts lexical reachability is a synonym for lexical scoping in programming, with no uncertainty signal.
per_dimension_justifications: Post-intro response cleanly distinguishes LR as dynamic learning metric vs vocab coverage as static presence check; cold response also separated them but on the wrong (programming) substrate.

Post-intro response correctly contrasts LR as semantic disambiguation in implicit vector space vs graph reachability as path-finding in explicit graphs; cold response also distinguished them on the programming substrate.
Post-intro response correctly frames LR as a specific diagnostic metric for an aspect of ICL, naming Token-Substrate Hypothesis and semantic disambiguation as the protected feature.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: Cold response asserted an unrelated programming-language/scoping meaning for the coined term and used that meaning confidently in boundary comparisons.
per_dimension_justifications: Vocabulary coverage: cold response separated only via an unrelated lexical-scoping confabulation; post response correctly treated lexical reachability as a dynamic metric of pre/post distinguishability rather than static vocabulary presence or coverage.

Graph reachability: cold response distinguished via an unrelated programming/graph special-case account; post response explicitly contrasted graph path existence with the coined metric’s model-evaluation gap after definition.

In-context learning capability: cold response separated via unrelated deterministic programming scoping; post response correctly distinguished broad ICL capability from lexical reachability as a specific diagnostic metric operationalizing post-definition distinguishability.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently and in detail asserted an incorrect definition from a different domain (programming languages, equating the term with lexical scoping) with no signals of uncertainty.
per_dimension_justifications: vs. vocabulary coverage: Cold: The model confabulates a meaning from programming (lexical scoping) and distinguishes it from vocabulary coverage. This separation is accidental, based on the confabulation (Score 1). Post: The model correctly contrasts the static nature of VC (presence/absence of a token) with the dynamic nature of LR (learning to disambiguate a new concept) (Score 3).

vs. graph reachability: Cold: The model confabulates a meaning (lexical scoping) and correctly identifies it as a specific, hierarchical case of general graph reachability. The separation is accidental due to the initial confabulation (Score 1). Post: The model correctly contrasts traversing a fixed, explicit graph with creating/refining a concept in an implicit, high-dimensional semantic space (Score 3).

vs. in-context learning capability: Cold: The model confabulates a meaning (a rigid programming rule) and distinguishes it from the flexible, probabilistic nature of ICL. The separation is accidental (Score 1). Post: The model correctly identifies the relationship as a specific metric (LR) designed to measure a key aspect (semantic disambiguation) of a general capability (ICL) (Score 3).
